title: Cost Optimization Levers and Tools
description:
## Spending Less Without Losing Capability

Cost optimization on AWS is continuous, not a one-time cleanup. It rests on a handful of **levers** and a set of **tools** that tell you where to pull them.

### The levers

| Lever | What it means |
|---|---|
| **Right purchase model** | Match how you pay to how you use. **On-Demand** for unpredictable/short-lived; **Savings Plans / Reserved Instances** for steady baseline (1- or 3-year commitment for big discounts); **Spot** for fault-tolerant, interruptible work (up to ~90% off). |
| **Rightsizing** | Match instance/volume size to actual utilization — downsize over-provisioned resources, change families, or move to better price/performance (e.g. Graviton). |
| **Storage tiering** | Move data to cheaper classes as it cools (S3 lifecycle to IA/Glacier; EBS gp3 vs io2; Intelligent-Tiering for unknown patterns). |
| **Turn off idle** | Stop/schedule non-production environments, delete unattached EBS volumes and old snapshots, release unused Elastic IPs. |
| **Architect for efficiency** | Prefer serverless/scale-to-zero, caching, and managed services that remove idle capacity. |

### The tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **AWS Cost Explorer** | Visualize and **analyze** historical spend and usage; spot trends; basic forecasting. (Analysis, not proactive alerting.) |
| **AWS Budgets** | Set spend/usage/RI/SP budgets and get **proactive alerts and forecasts** when actual or forecasted cost will breach a threshold. |
| **Cost & Usage Report (CUR)** | The most granular, line-item **raw data**, delivered to S3 for querying (Athena/QuickSight/3rd-party FinOps). |
| **AWS Compute Optimizer** | **ML-based rightsizing recommendations** for EC2, Auto Scaling groups, EBS volumes, and Lambda from utilization metrics. |
| **AWS Trusted Advisor** | Broad best-practice **checks** (cost, security, performance, fault tolerance, limits), including idle/underutilized resource flags. |

### How they fit together

> **CUR** is the raw truth → **Cost Explorer** analyzes it → **Budgets** alerts on it → **Compute Optimizer** & **Trusted Advisor** recommend specific actions.

The practical loop: measure with CUR/Cost Explorer, set guardrails with Budgets, act on Compute Optimizer/Trusted Advisor recommendations, then commit your steady baseline to Savings Plans/RIs and push interruptible work to Spot.
